questions.question: Which part of a Java program contains the statements that are executed?
questions.explanation: The main method is where execution begins.

questions.question: If the class name is Hello, what should the file name be?
questions.explanation: The file name should match the class name and end with .java.

questions.question: Which symbol ends a Java statement?
questions.explanation: A semicolon ends a Java statement.

questions.question: Which command prints and moves to the next line?
questions.explanation: println prints and then moves to the next line.

questions.question: Which command prints without moving to the next line?
questions.explanation: print keeps the output on the same line.

questions.question: Which data type stores text?
questions.explanation: String is used to store text.

questions.question: Which of the following is an integral data type?
questions.explanation: int is used for whole numbers.

questions.question: Which data type is best for the value 45.75?
questions.explanation: Decimal values can be stored in double.

questions.question: Which data type has range -128 to 127?
questions.explanation: byte has the range -128 to 127.

questions.question: Which data type uses 2 bytes among the given whole number types?
questions.explanation: short uses 2 bytes.

questions.question: What is a variable?
questions.explanation: A variable stores a value using a name.

questions.question: What does initialization mean?
options: C. Assigning a value to a variable
questions.correct_answer: C. Assigning a value to a variable
questions.explanation: Initialization gives a variable its value.

questions.question: In int age = 13;, which part is the variable name?
questions.explanation: age is the name of the variable.

questions.question: In int age = 13;, which part is the literal?
questions.explanation: 13 is the literal value.

questions.question: Which operator gives the remainder after division?
questions.explanation: The modulus operator gives the remainder.

questions.question: Which of the following is an arithmetic operator?
questions.explanation: The symbol + is an arithmetic operator.

questions.question: In 5 + 3, what are 5 and 3 called?
questions.explanation: Operands are the values on which the operator works.

questions.question: What is the operator in 100 + 800?
questions.explanation: The operator is the symbol that performs the action.

questions.question: What is an expression?
options: B. A piece of code that produces a value
questions.correct_answer: B. A piece of code that produces a value
questions.explanation: An expression gives a value when evaluated.

questions.question: Which of the following is a statement?
questions.explanation: This is a complete command ending with a semicolon.

questions.question: Which symbol is the simple assignment operator?
questions.explanation: The simple assignment operator is =.

questions.question: What does x = 25 do?
questions.explanation: The right side value is assigned to the variable on the left side.

questions.question: Which of these is a binary operator?
questions.explanation: A binary operator works on two operands.

questions.question: Which brackets define code blocks in Java?
questions.explanation: Curly brackets define code blocks.

questions.question: What is the output of System.out.println(10 + 5);?
questions.explanation: The expression is evaluated and then printed.

questions.question: What is the value of 17 % 5?
questions.explanation: 17 divided by 5 leaves remainder 2.

questions.question: What is the value of 18 / 3?
questions.explanation: 18 divided by 3 equals 6.

questions.question: What is the value of 6 * 4?
questions.explanation: 6 multiplied by 4 equals 24.

questions.question: What is the value of 20 - 7?
questions.explanation: 20 minus 7 equals 13.

questions.question: Where should output statements usually be written in these beginner programs?
questions.explanation: The print statements are written inside the main method.
